pattern: Sorting by value turns each node's reachable set into a contiguous value window, so shortest hops are found by always jumping to the farthest still-reachable node from the current frontier.
hints: From any node you can reach, in one step, every node whose value lies within the threshold - a contiguous band once values are sorted.
Shortest distance is a greedy interval-cover: from your current reachable span, extend to the farthest node any boundary node can reach.

Work in sorted order, track the current covered interval, and count how many one-step expansions are needed to bring the target inside it; map answers back to original indices and return -1 when stuck.

edge_inputs.description: mix of reachable, two-hop, and disconnected
maxDiff zero only links equal values
must chain through several intermediate values

stress.description: unsorted random values, several queries - brute BFS stays cheap at this size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    maxDiff = rng.randint(0, 8)
    q = rng.randint(1, 60)
    queries = [[rng.randrange(n), rng.randrange(n)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [n, nums, maxDiff, queries]
stress.note: small maxDiff produces both reachable and -1 outcomes

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def pathExistenceQueries(self, n, nums, maxDiff, queries):
        order = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: nums[i])
        pos = [0] * n
        sv = [0] * n
        for p, idx in enumerate(order):
            pos[idx] = p
            sv[p] = nums[idx]
        ans = []
        for u, v in queries:
            pu, pv = pos[u], pos[v]
            if pu == pv:
                ans.append(0)
                continue
            lo, hi = (pu, pv) if pu < pv else (pv, pu)
            steps = 0
            cur_lo, cur_hi = lo, lo
            reached = False
            while True:
                if cur_lo <= hi <= cur_hi:
                    reached = True
                    break
                left = bisect.bisect_left(sv, sv[cur_lo] - maxDiff)
                right = bisect.bisect_right(sv, sv[cur_hi] + maxDiff) - 1
                if left == cur_lo and right == cur_hi:
                    break
                cur_lo, cur_hi = left, right
                steps += 1
            ans.append(steps if reached else -1)
        return ans
